Brittany uses 1/4 ounce of shampoo every other day, which means she uses [amount] ounces per week. A leap year has 366 days, so she washes her hair [number] times in a year. To calculate the total amount of shampoo she needs, multiply the number of washes by 1/4 ounce. This gives the total ounces needed for the year, which can then be divided by the size of the shampoo bottles she buys, which are 14 ounces each.

To determine how many bottles she needs, divide the total ounces of shampoo used in the year by 14. If the result is not a whole number, round up to the next whole number because she cannot purchase a fraction of a bottle. This will give the total number of bottles [number] she needs to buy.
